results.rendered_html: maugdha (w.r.  Sāh. ;  Uttarar. ) or maugdhya ( ib. ;  Kathās.  &c.), n. (fr. mugdha), simplicity, innocence, inexperience, a feminine grace (asking a lover to tell what is already known); charm, beauty,  MW.

results.rendered_html: maugdhya (von mugdha) n. Einfalt, Unschuld, Unerfahrenheit, reizende Naivetät  KATHĀS. 20,57 .  Spr. 3244 .  UTTARARĀMAC. 107,6  (neuere Ausg.). ajñānādiva yā pṛcchā pratītasyāpi vastunaḥ . vallabhasya puraḥ proktaṃ maugdhyaṃ tattattvavedibhiḥ ..  SĀH. D. 148 . silliness  BALLANTYNE.
